s Cape Code Host Memory Leaks It looks like we are leaking memory in Cape Code where we are retaining classes when we open models. To replicate this: Start up reloadAllAccessors cd $PTII $PTII / bin / reloadAllAccessors & [$[Get Code]] Start up jvisu

reloadAllAccessors & [$[Get Code]] Start up jvisualvm [$[Get Code]] Find the Java process running ptolemy.vergil.basic.imprt.accessor.ReloadAccessors See Memory Leaks (ptexternal wiki) for how to use jvisualvm, especially Dealing with memory leaks i

jvisualvm, especially Dealing with memory leaks in Kepler In the monitor tab, note that the amount of memory increases and that GC is taking lots of time: Press Heap Dump In the Heap Dump, press the Class button then press on Class Name to sort by c

Class button then press on Class Name to sort by classes and look for the ptolemy.vergil.basic packages In the above, we are not leaking ActorGraphFrame or BasicGraphFrame, but we are leaking some actions Click on ptolemy.vergil.basic.BasicGraphContr

ions Click on ptolemy.vergil.basic.BasicGraphController$OpenBaseClassAction In the References section, right click and select Show Nearest GC Root: In the above WebCamDiscoveryService is highlighted. At this point, I ran out of time. See Dealing wit
